_description: Phase-1 NHEFS + NHANES I variable registry. Curated for the canonical smoking-cessation → weight-gain research question and the minimal set of covariates needed to reproduce the Hernán/Robins Ch 12 result. Track B will expand this registry to cover all 4 NHEFS follow-up waves and additional NHANES I baseline modules.

_conventions.source: Which file the variable lives in. Matches a key in merge_keys.json -> datasets.
_conventions.wave: baseline | f1_1982 | f2_1986 | f3_1987 | f4_1992 | all (see wave_mapping.json)
_conventions.roles: Suggested analytic role(s): identifier, demographic, outcome, exposure, covariate, weight, design, flag. Descriptive — not enforced.
_conventions.missing_values: List of string sentinels to replace with NaN. Applied by M3 after .str.strip(). Overrides the width-based defaults in nhefs_missing_conventions.json.

seqn.label: NCHS sample sequence number (canonical)
seqn.notes: Canonical post-harmonization name. Built from SEQN (NHANES I), SEQNUM (NHEFS vital/mort/later waves), or SEQNUM1 (NHEFS 1982 interview). See merge_keys.json.

REVRACE.label: Revised race (NHEFS harmonized coding)
REVRACE.notes: Exact coding verified against readme. NHEFS uses REVRACE as the recoded race field; NHANES I uses N1BM0103 with different codes ({1:White, 2:Negro, 3:Other}) — do not merge blindly.

NEXAMAGE.label: Age at NHANES I examination
NEXAMAGE.notes: Range 25-74 by NHEFS cohort definition. Primary 'baseline age' variable for Phase-1 analyses.

MARTSTAT.label: Marital status of subject when last interviewed

N1BM0112.label: Highest grade of regular school ever attended
N1BM0112.notes: NCHS education codes (see DU4111_F.sas PROC FORMAT BM0112F). Common groupings: <HS (10-32), HS (33), some college (34-41), college+ (42+). Derived categorical 'education' var is built in M4 preprocessing.

survey_design.description: Survey sampling design variables for weighted analyses (Phase 2)

STRATA.label: NHEFS sampling stratum
STRATA.notes: Used with PSU for Taylor-series variance estimation in complex survey analyses. Not used in Phase 1 (unweighted analysis); wired up in Phase 2.

vital_status.description: Vital status and interview disposition per wave

CVITALST.label: Current vital status of subject
CVITALST.notes: Values 3-6 all indicate death. Phase-1 all-cause mortality flag is derived as CVITALST != 1.

INTDIS82.label: Interview disposition, 1982-84 NHEFS
INTDIS82.notes: Indicates whether the subject was interviewed in 1982-84, and if not, why not (deceased, refused, lost, etc.).

mortality.description: Date and cause of death (populated only for decedents)

CADTHRC3.label: Cause of death recode (34 groups)
CADTHRC3.notes: Easier analytic grouping than raw ICD-9. Used for cause-specific mortality in Phase-2 survival analyses.

physical_baseline.description: Physical measurements at NHANES I baseline examination

N1BM0255.label: Weight in pounds (XXX.XX, implied decimal)
N1BM0255.notes: Width 5, 2 implied decimal places. Divide raw integer by 100 to get pounds. Prefer N1BM0260 (kilograms) for analysis.

N1BM0260.label: Weight in kilograms (XXX.XX, implied decimal)
N1BM0260.notes: Width 5, 2 implied decimal places. Divide raw integer by 100 to get kg. PRIMARY baseline weight variable — used for the wt71 side of wt82_71.

N1BM0266.label: Height in centimeters (XXX.X, implied decimal)
N1BM0266.notes: Width 4, 1 implied decimal. Divide raw by 10 to get cm. Primary baseline height for BMI calculation.

health_conditions_baseline.description: Self-reported baseline health conditions (DU4081 adult medical history)

N1AH0435.label: Heart trouble or high blood pressure (composite)

smoking_baseline.description: Baseline smoking variables from NHANES I cardio/resp supplement (DU4091). LIMITED COVERAGE: ~6,913 subjects, ~half of NHEFS cohort. Prefer smoking_1982 variables for primary analysis.

N1GM0378.label: Ever smoked at least 100 cigarettes in lifetime
N1GM0378.coverage_warning: Only ~6,913 subjects received the cardio/resp supplement. See nhefs_missing_conventions.json structural_missingness.

N1GM0384.label: Average cigarettes per day (when currently smoking)
N1GM0384.notes: Codes 90/98 = '(87+ cigarettes per day)' per PROC FORMAT. Topcoded, treat as continuous with awareness.

smoking_1982.description: 1982-84 interview smoking status. PRIMARY source for Phase-1 'qsmk' (quit smoking) derivation because it has far better cohort coverage (~12,220) than the DU4091 baseline supplement.

L1.label: Subject ever smoked 100+ cigarettes in lifetime

L2a.label: Subject smokes cigarettes now (1982)
L2a.notes: Combined with L1: qsmk = 1 if L1=1 (ever smoker) AND L2a=2 (not now) — i.e., 'quit by 1982'. qsmk = 0 if L1=1 AND L2a=1 — 'still smoking in 1982'. qsmk undefined if L1=2 (never smoker).

L2b.label: Subject stopped smoking cigarettes in past year

L4P1.label: Subject stopped smoking cigarettes at least 1 year (1st period)

L5P1.label: Age subject stopped smoking cigarettes > 1 yr (1st period)
L5P1.notes: Used with NEXAMAGE and NDOBYR to determine whether the quit happened between baseline (1971-75) and 1982. This is what defines the 'quit smoking between NHANES I and 1982' variable.

L7.label: Number of cigarettes per day subject smokes now

L9.label: Total average cigarettes per day subject smoked (all years)
L9.notes: Maps to the Hernán 'smokeintensity' covariate in the textbook nhefs.csv. Higher intensity at baseline predicts both quit probability and weight change — classic confounder.

physical_1982.description: Physical measurements taken in person at the 1982-84 NHEFS interview. Critical for computing wt82_71 weight change outcome.

PMR5b1.label: Weight measurement, 1st reading (1982)
PMR5b1.notes: Measured, not self-reported. This is the wt82 side of the classic wt82_71 = wt82 - wt71 outcome. Check codebook PDF for exact units (likely pounds, width TBD).

PMR5b2.label: Weight measurement, 2nd reading (1982)
PMR5b2.notes: Some subjects received two readings. M4 averages both when available.

PMR4cAVS.label: Average of 2nd/3rd BP readings — systolic
PMR4cAVS.notes: Pre-averaged by CDC. Prefer over single-reading PMR4c1SY when available.

behaviors_1982.description: Self-reported lifestyle behaviors at 1982 (used as covariates for the smoking-cessation → weight-gain analysis)

K4.label: Current weight of subject (self-reported)
K4.notes: Self-report. Prefer measured PMR5b1 when available.

M1.label: Had at least 12 alcoholic beverages in 1 year

Q3.label: Follows a regular physical exercise program
Q3.notes: Rough proxy for the Hernán 'active'/'exercise' covariates.

phase_1_research_question.title: Does smoking cessation between 1971-75 and 1982 cause weight gain by 1982?
phase_1_research_question.reference: Hernán & Robins, Causal Inference: What If, Chapter 12
phase_1_research_question.expected_effect: ~3.4 kg average weight gain among quitters vs non-quitters (after adjusting for baseline confounders)

variable_roles.exposure: qsmk — derived from L1 (ever smoker) + L2a (smokes now in 1982). qsmk=1 if L1=1 AND L2a=2. Defined only for L1=1 (ever smokers).
variable_roles.outcome: wt82_71 = wt82 - wt71. wt82 from PMR5b1/PMR5b2 (measured in 1982). wt71 from N1BM0260 (baseline kg).
confounders: N1BM0260 (baseline weight — the wt71 variable is both part of the outcome AND a confounder, per Hernán)
